meta.title: V167 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every Mercedes V167 engine — OM654, OM656, M256, M176, M177. Real UK costs, honest reliability data, and the OM654 chain risk explained. Get free quotes from 25+ vetted Mercedes specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes V167 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned V167 engine typically costs £2,500–£15,000 supply only [MB‑QUOTE], depending on engine — the OM654 four‑cylinder diesel runs £2,500–£4,500, while the M177 V8 in the GLE 63 AMG can cost £8,000–£15,000. Fitting adds £780–£1,430. Use our calculator for a personalised quote.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes V167 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M256 3.0‑litre six‑cylinder petrol with EQ Boost (GLE 450) is the most advanced and generally reliable choice — it's smooth, responsive, and benefits from the mild‑hybrid system. The OM656 six‑cylinder diesel is robust but suffers from oil‑cooler seal leaks; the OM654 four‑cylinder diesel is economical but needs chain monitoring.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes V167?

acceptedAnswer.text: OM654 diesels suffer timing‑chain stretch; OM656 diesels have oil‑cooler seal leaks; M256 EQ Boost engines can experience ISG (integrated starter‑generator) failure; M176/M177 V8 petrols can develop turbo coolant line failures. A recall (22V‑614) affects the M256 ISG. Regular servicing is critical across all engines.

It depends on the engine. The M256 is generally excellent but watch for ISG issues. The OM656 diesel is robust but has a well‑documented oil‑cooler seal weakness. The OM654 needs chain monitoring. The M176/M177 are high‑performance engines that demand meticulous maintenance. Overall, a well‑maintained V167 can be a dependable luxury SUV.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes V167 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: No engine is an outright "avoid" — but an OM654 without documented timing‑chain replacement is one we'd approach with caution. Similarly, a high‑mileage GLE 63 AMG without full service history warrants careful inspection. Always get a specialist pre‑purchase inspection.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my V167 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Key warning signs include: cold‑start rattle (OM654 chain), burning oil smell (OM656 seals), warning lights relating to the 48‑volt system (M256 ISG), coolant loss with no visible leak (M176/M177 coolant lines), or a top‑end tick that worsens (M177 lifters). If you experience any of these, get a diagnosis immediately — early intervention saves thousands.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes V167 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the car's value. Against a well‑kept £35,000 V167, a £5,500 OM656 recon is 16% of the car's value — safe if the car is otherwise sound. Against a tired £18,000 example, the same recon is 31% — still safe but approaching our 70% Mercedes threshold. Use our 70% rule as your guide.

mainEntity.name: How long do Mercedes V167 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With proper maintenance, the M256 petrol can exceed 150,000 miles, and the OM656 diesel can reach 200,000+ miles with regular oil changes. The OM654 diesel is also capable of high mileage but requires chain monitoring. The M176/M177 V8s are durable but demand expensive preventative maintenance.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the V167 and C167?

acceptedAnswer.text: Both share the same platform and engines. The V167 is the 5‑door SUV (2019–2023, this page), while the C167 is the SUV coupé (2019–2023). Costs vary slightly by body style due to labour access — see the C167's own Generation Page for specifics.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes V167 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate (visible through the windscreen) and in the vehicle documentation. The code is stamped on the engine block — on OM654/OM656 it's on the front left, on M256/M176/M177 it's on the right side. Tell us your variant and registration year, and we'll identify it for you.

hero.h1: Mercedes GLE V167 Engines — The Complete UK Guide

engineDatabase.h2: Mercedes GLE V167 Engine Codes — The Complete Database

confidenceScore.title: Confidence Score
confidenceScore.text: OM654 and M256 ratings: 95% confidence, inherited from the Model Page where one exists. OM656, M274, M176, and M177: Expert‑knowledge tier, derived from cross‑referencing specialist and owner sources — confirm against the Model Page before publishing.

overview.intro: The V167 is the fourth generation of the Mercedes‑Benz GLE‑Class, a large luxury SUV produced from 2019 to 2023. Built on the Mercedes‑Benz MHA platform, it succeeded the W166 and introduced the EQ Boost 48‑volt mild‑hybrid system across several powertrains. The V167 is available with rear‑wheel drive or 4MATIC all‑wheel drive, and offers a broad engine range spanning four‑cylinder diesels, six‑cylinder petrols with EQ Boost, V8 petrols, and plug‑in hybrid variants. A facelift in 2023 brought design updates and engine enhancements.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: Mercedes‑Benz MHA • Predecessor: W166 • Successor: V167 facelift (2023–present) • Sibling body styles in this generation: C167 GLE Coupé (2019–2023).
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: No V167‑specific enquiry figure is available; OM654 diesel enquiries dominate the broader Mercedes SUV parc, with 520 UK requests in 2025 [MB‑VERIFIED].

rows.ownershipVerdict: Watch the Chain — tensioner is the key risk
Six‑Cylinder Torque — smooth, but seals need watching
Best Petrol — refined six‑cylinder with EQ Boost
V8 Grunt — fast, but hot‑vee complexity
AMG Tax — high performance, high cost
Performance Hybrid — AMG performance, hybrid complexity

economicsRule.title: The V167 rule
economicsRule.text: A £4,000–£7,000 M256 recon against a £20,000–£50,000 V167 sits at 8–35% of the car’s value — generally safe. Against a sub‑£20,000 example, the same recon can brush our 70% Mercedes threshold — we’ll honestly tell you to walk away. Against a well‑kept £40,000+ example, the same recon drops to a routine 10–18% — a straightforward repair decision. Value the car first.

keyTakeaways.question: Which V167 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: OM654 2.0 diesel — cheapest recon and simple repairs.

keyTakeaways.question: Which V167 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: M177 4.0 AMG — AMG parts and labour are eye‑wateringly expensive.

keyTakeaways.question: Which V167 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: GLE 450 (M256) — six‑cylinder refinement without V8 running costs.

problems.title: OM654 Timing‑Chain Stretch
problems.affectedModels: V167 GLE 300 d, GLE 350 de (OM654 diesel).
problems.rootCause: The OM654 has a rear‑mounted timing chain that can stretch over time. Cold‑start rattle is the classic early warning — if ignored, the chain can jump and cause catastrophic engine damage.
problems.recommendation: At the first sign of cold‑start rattle on an OM654, get the chain inspected immediately — a £600–£1,500 preventative repair is far cheaper than the £2,500+ replacement that follows if ignored.

cta.label: Compare OM654 engine prices for your V167 →

problems.title: M256 ISG (Integrated Starter‑Generator) Failure
problems.affectedModels: V167 GLE 450, AMG GLE 53 (M256 EQ Boost petrol).
problems.rootCause: The integrated starter‑generator (ISG) in the M256 EQ Boost system can fail, leading to loss of the 48‑volt mild‑hybrid functionality, warning lights, and potential drivability issues. A recall (22V‑614) affects the entire W167 GLE range.
problems.recommendation: If you own an M256‑powered V167, check if the ISG recall (22V‑614) has been performed. A £1,000–£2,500 repair is cheaper than the £4,000+ replacement that follows a full failure.

cta.label: Compare M256 engine prices for your V167 →

problems.title: M176 / M177 Turbo Coolant Line Failure
problems.affectedModels: V167 GLE 580, GLE 63 AMG, GLE 63 S AMG (M176/M177 V8).
problems.rootCause: The hot‑vee design places the turbos in the V of the engine, and the coolant lines can degrade with heat cycling, leading to coolant loss and potential turbo damage.
problems.recommendation: If you own a V8‑powered V167, have the coolant system inspected annually — a £1,000–£2,500 repair is far cheaper than the £6,000+ replacement that follows a catastrophic failure.

cta.label: Compare M176 / M177 engine prices for your V167 →

problems.title: OM656 Oil‑Cooler Seal Leak
problems.affectedModels: V167 GLE 350 d, GLE 400 d (OM656 six‑cylinder diesel).
problems.rootCause: The oil‑cooler O‑rings in the V of the OM656 engine can degrade with heat cycling, causing oil leaks that can drip onto the exhaust and create a burning smell. Labour is intensive as the intake manifold must be removed.
problems.recommendation: If you notice a burning oil smell or oil residue in the engine V, have the oil‑cooler seals replaced immediately — an £800–£1,500 repair is cheaper than the £3,500+ replacement that follows if the engine runs low on oil.

cta.label: Compare OM656 engine prices for your V167 →

OM654 and M256 costs carried forward from the EM data's Table A.
